Journal.Title: Langmuir : the ACS journal of surfaces and colloids

Article.ArticleTitle: Insights into the Pseudocapacitive Mechanism of Selenium-Doped Nickel Cobaltite for Supercapacitors with Improved Electrochemical Performance.

Abstract.AbstractText: Doping engineering has been widely regarded as an efficient way to optimize the electrochemical characteristics of transition metal oxides. In this study, we have adopted a facile hydrothermal method to prepare a multifunctional material, which is uniformly composed of Se-doped NiCo2O4 and significant amounts of SeO2 nanophase (referred to as NCO/Se) as a promising electrode material. The influence of selenium doping is systematically studied based on both experimental and theoretical approaches. As a result, the optimized selenium-doped NCO/Se exhibits significantly enhanced electrochemical performance (a specific capacity of 333.9 mA h g-1 at 1 A g-1) in comparison to the pristine NiCo2O4 (a specific capacity of 188.1 mA h g-1 at 1 A g-1). Density functional theory calculations further validate that Se doping effectively narrows the band gap, elevates the charge carrier concentration, and optimizes the adsorption energy of OH- ions, which consequently enhances electrical conductivity, facilitates ion diffusion, and accelerates the electrochemical kinetics. Additionally, the Se-doped crumpled sea-urchin-like morphology contributes to increased specific surface area, offers abundant ion transport pathways, and significantly improves the availability of active sites. Furthermore, a high-performance NCO/Se//activated carbon supercapacitor device was constructed, achieving an outstanding energy density of 59.8 W h kg-1 at a power density of 800 W kg-1. After 10,000 charge-discharge cycles, the device preserves 84.8% of its original capacitance, indicating outstanding cycling stability. This study offers valuable theoretical and experimental insights into the development of advanced atom-doped transition metal oxides for electrochemical energy storage applications.
